(Gilgameš said to Enkidu:) 'Let ... [...].

liš-bi-⸢ra?⸣ [...]

'Let them smash [...],

r ii 4'4'

a-šam-ša₂-a-⸢tu⸣ [...]

'the dust storms [...].'

r ii 5'5'

den-ki-du₃ pa-⸢a⸣-[šu₂ ...]

Enkidu [...] his mouth [...]:

r ii 6'6'

ki-i ni-⸢il⸣-[lak ...]

'How can we go [...]?

r ii 7'7'

KASKAL-MIN ši-i [...]

'That road [...].

r ii 8'8'

na-ṣir giš[TIR ...]

'The guardian of the forest [...].

r ii 9'9'

dḫum-ba-ba [...]

'Humbaba, [...].

r ii 10'10'

i-šem-me-e-⸢ma⸣ [...]

'He hears [...];

r ii 11'11'

man-nu-um-ma [...]

'who [...]?

r ii 12'12'

dIŠKUR 1en [...]

'Adad is first, [...];

r ii 13'13'

man-nu ša₂ i-ge-⸢er⸣-[ru-šu₂ ...]

'who is there who would oppose him [...]?

r ii 14'14'

aš₂-šu₂ šul-lu-mu giš⸢EREN⸣ [...]

'For the sake of keeping the cedar safe, [...].

r ii 15'15'

u₃ a-rid gišTIR-šu₂ [...]

'And he who ventures into his forest, [...].'

r ii 16'16'

dGIŠ-gim₂-maš pa-a-šu₂ DU₃-ma i-⸢qab-bi⸣ [...]

Gilgameš opened his mouth to speak, [...]:

r ii 17'17'

am-mi₃-ni ib-ri pi-is-nu-[qiš ...]

'Why, my friend, like a weakling [...]?

r ii 18'18'

u₃ pi-i-ka ir-ma-am-ma ⸢tu⸣-[lam-man x x]

'And your mouth slackened, troubling [...].

r ii 19'19'

a-me-lut-ti ma-nu-⸢u₂⸣ [...]

'As for man, [...] are numbered,

r ii 20'20'

mim-mu-u₂ ⸢e⸣-te-ep-pu-šu ⸢TU₁₅⸣-[MEŠ?-ma]

'all that he kept doing is (just) wind.

r ii 21'21'

x x [x] ⸢ul i⸣-ba-aš₂-ša₂-a x [...]

'... [...] they do not exist ... [...];

r ii 22'22'

⸢ta-ʾa-al⸣-dam-ma tar-ba-a ⸢i⸣-[na x x]

'You were born and grew up in [...].

r ii 23'23'

iš-⸢ḫu?-tu?-ka?⸣-a-⸢ma⸣ la-ab-bi ⸢ka⸣-[la-ma ...]

'Lions feared you, [...] all,

r ii 24'24'

u₃ ⸢eṭ⸣-lu-tu ⸢iʾ₂⸣-bu-tu₄ [...]

'young men too ran away [...].

r ii 25'25'

mu-du-⸢u₂ lib₃⸣-ba-ka tu-qu-⸢un⸣-[tu₂ x x]

'Your very wise heart [...] battle;

r ii 26'26'

⸢al⸣-kam-ma ib-ri ana kiš-⸢kat₂⸣-[te-e ...]

'come, my friend, [...] to the forge.'

r ii 27'27'

[x] ⸢kiš-kat₂⸣-te-e [...]

[...] the forge [...]

r ii 28'28'

[...] x [...]

r iii 1'r 1'

[... 2]-⸢šu₂⸣

(Gilgameš said to the people of Uruk:) '[...] 2 times [...].

r iii 2'2'

[... li]-⸢ib⸣-ši

'[...] let there be [...],

r iii 3'3'

[... fri-mat—dNIN].⸢SUN₂⸣

'[...] Rimat-Ninsun.'

r iii 4'4'

[... i?]-⸢mal?-lik?⸣

[...] he (Enkidu) gave advice [...],

r iii 5'5'

[...]-tu₄

[...] ...:

r iii 6'6'

[...] ⸢giš⸣EREN

'[...] of cedar,

r iii 7'7'

[...] a-ma-ri

'[...] for seeing,

r iii 8'8'

[...]-⸢šu₂ rit⸣-pa-ša₂

'[...], his [...] are very wide.

r iii 9'9'

[...] na-pi-is-su₂ mu-u₂-tu₂

'[...], his breath is death.

r iii 10'10'

[...] ⸢rim⸣-mat gišTIR-šu₂

'[...] the murmurs of his forest;

r iii 11'11'

[...] a-na gišTIR-šu₂

'[...] into his forest?

r iii 12'12'

[...] ša₂-nu-u₂

'[...] second;

r iii 13'13'

[... i]-na di₂-gi₃-gi₃

'[...] among the Igigu?

r iii 14'14'

[... pul-ḫa]-⸢a⸣-ta ša₂ UN-MEŠ i-šim-šu₂ dEN.LIL₂

'[...] Ellil destined him [...] the terrifying of the people.

r iii 15'15'

[... giš]⸢TIR-šu₂⸣ i-ṣab-bat-su lu-uʾ-tu

'[...] his forest, weakness will seize him.'

r iii 16'16'

[it-bu]-⸢u₂⸣-[ma] ma-⸢li⸣-ke-e rab-bu-tu

The senior advisers arose,

r iii 17'17'

⸢ṭe₃?-e?-me? u₂⸣-tar-ru a-na dGIŠ-gim₂-maš

responding with (their) opinion to Gilgameš:

r iii 18'18'

⸢ṣe⸣-eḫ-re-e-ti d⸢GIŠ-gim₂⸣-[maš] ⸢ŠA₃⸣-ka na-ši-ka

'You are young, Gilgameš, your heart is ruling you,

r iii 19'19'

⸢u₃⸣ mim-ma ša₂ ta-ta-mu-u₂ <<x>> ⸢ul⸣ ti-i-de

'and you do not know all that you talked about.

r iii 20'20'

dḫum-ba-ba rig-ma-šu₂ a-bu-bu

'Humbaba, his voice is the flood,

r iii 21'21'

pi-i-šu dGIRA₂um-⸢ma⸣ na-pi-is-su mu-u₂-tu

'his speech is fire, his breath is death.

r iii 22'22'

⸢i⸣-šem-me-e-[ma?] a-na 60-šu ⸢DANNA⸣ [...] ⸢giš⸣TIR-šu₂

'He hears to 60 leagues [...] of his forest;

r iii 23'23'

a-ri-du gišTIR-šu₂ ⸢i⸣-[ṣab-bat-su lu-uʾ]-⸢tu⸣

'he who ventures into his forest, weakness will seize him.

r iii 24'24'

man-nu ša₂ ur-ra-du [x x gišTIR]-⸢šu₂⸣

'Who is there who would venture [...] his forest?

r iii 25'25'

man-nu ša₂ i-ge-[er-ru-šu₂ ...]

'Who is there who would oppose him [...]?

r iii 26'26'

dIŠKUR 1!(AN)en [...]

'Adad is first, [...].

r iii 27'27'

aš₂-šu₂ ⸢šul⸣-lu-mu giš⸢EREN⸣ [...]

'For the sake of keeping the cedar safe, [...].'

r iii 28'28'

iš-me-e-ma [...]

He (Gilgameš) heard [...],

r iii 29'29'

⸢ip⸣-pa-lis-⸢ma⸣ [...]

he looked and [...].

r iii 30'30'

x x x [...]
